channel.title: The Valve Wire Weekly
channel.description: A weekly podcast covering the latest in transcatheter valve technology — TAVR, mitral repair and replacement, tricuspid therapies, clinical trials, and industry analysis. Balanced, evidence-based analysis with expert skepticism. Hosted by E. Nolan Beckett.
channel.itunes:author: E. Nolan Beckett
channel.itunes:summary: Weekly analysis of transcatheter valve technology developments for cardiac surgeons, interventional cardiologists, trainees, patients, and industry stakeholders. We present balanced, circumspect analysis — always questioning whether therapies have gotten ahead of the evidence.

item.title: The Valve Wire Weekly - 2026-03-25
item.description: Weekly podcast covering transcatheter valve technology developments for the week ending 2026-03-25.
item.content:encoded:
The Valve Wire Weekly - 2026-03-25

Duration: 21:18 | Hosts: E. Nolan Beckett, MD & Claire Marchand, MBA

Timestamps

  • [00:48] Introduction
  • [01:26] Disclaimer
  • [01:41] Top Stories
  • [04:11] Aortic Valve (TAVR/TAVI)
  • [06:48] Mitral Valve (Repair & Replacement)
  • [09:20] Tricuspid Valve (Repair & Replacement)
  • [10:54] Clinical Trials Update
  • [12:56] Surgical vs. Transcatheter
  • [15:03] Market & Industry
  • [17:44] Weekend News
  • [19:12] Closing Thoughts

Summary

Welcome to The Valve Wire Weekly, the podcast covering the latest in transcatheter valve technology. I'm Dr. Nolan Beckett, your host and structural heart specialist. This is our very first episode, and what a week to launch — we've got historic FDA approvals, concerning durability data, and some fascinating new research. Thanks Nolan, and welcome everyone! I'm Claire Marchand, bringing you the market and industry perspective. This week really had everything — from JenaValve's groundbreaking approval for aortic regurgitation to some sobering long-term data on mitral repair that's got the whole field talking.

Articles & Sources

  • JenaValve received FDA approval
  • Weighted pooled data from 33 studies
  • SURVIV
  • TAVR demonstrates significantly lower 30-day stroke rates (3.0%) compared to surgical AVR
  • eight independent risk factors for kidney injury after TAVR
  • tirzepatide, a GLP-1 receptor agonist, may reduce post-procedural valve dysfunction
  • TAVR shows an 87.5% device success rate
  • virtual reality technology improved spatial understanding in TAVR procedures
  • only 13.2% survival at 10 years, but acceptable transcatheter heart valve durability
  • 7-year health status data
  • complete AV block occurring 17 months after TAVR
  • significant discrepancies between echocardiographic and invasive gradient measurements
  • different aortic stenosis phenotypes in 15,233 TAVR patients
  • comprehensive review of valve selection in small aortic annuli
  • feasibility of detecting aortic stenosis using smartphone-recorded heart sounds
  • EXPANDed studies on left ventricular reverse remodeling
  • similar patient-reported outcomes improvements in both sexes following valve-in-valve procedures
  • valve-in-valve transcatheter mitral valve replacement could save $418,001 per death averted
  • multimodality imaging approaches to clarify mechanisms of unilateral pulmonary edema
  • hypoxia due to right-to-left shunting through patent foramen ovale
  • right heart strain changes after transcatheter tricuspid valve replacement
  • prevalence and impact of diabetes mellitus
  • NCT02701283
  • NCT07317804
  • NCT05149755
  • NCT07477002
  • NCT07411768
  • NCT07453407
  • NCT04198870
  • NCT05051033
  • NCT03706833
  • NCT07069673
  • NCT03242642
  • NCT04101357
  • NCT03433274
  • NCT07470788
  • NCT03904147
  • NCT04097145
  • NCT04482062
  • NCT05071768
  • poor overall survival regardless of approach
  • high adherence to heart team recommendations (89%)
  • early cusp thickening in 22% of patients and reduced cusp motion in 40%
  • updated National Coverage Analysis for TAVR
  • 2025 ESC/EACTS Guidelines for valvular heart disease
  • consensus recommendations for transcatheter pulmonary valve replacement
  • "affordable Made-in-India TAVR systems for high-risk heart patients"
  • "morning exercise may have added benefits for the heart"
  • TAVR valve thrombosis review: detection methods and long-term implications
  • Extended-length sheaths for TAVR in complex anatomy
  • 3D echo-guided tricuspid replacement technical report
  • Aortic valve prosthesis choice in dialysis patients: Medicare data review
  • MANTA closure device in EVAR: multicenter registry (279 groins)
  • PASCAL Ace device for left ventricular outflow obstruction case report
  • Femoral artery occlusion after MANTA deployment: series of 34 patients
  • Percutaneous retrieval of embolized Amulet device: complex case report
  • "Suicide left ventricle" following aortic valve interventions: mechanistic review
  • Prophylactic chimney stenting in TAVR: Mexican single-center series
  • Lipoprotein(a) levels and long-term survival after TAVR: Polish study (82 patients)
  • CT-based annular dimensions in transcatheter mitral valve replacement
  • Simultaneous TAVR and endovascular aneurysm repair case report
  • MitraClip nursing protocols for mirror-image dextrocardia patients
  • Refractory ventricular tachycardia storm: 42 ICD shocks in 24 hours
  • FDA approval of Trilogy TAVR System for severe aortic regurgitation

Subscribe: The Valve Wire Newsletter
